authors_text: Daniel Louzada Fernandes,Marcos Henrique Fonseca Ribeiro,Fabio Ribeiro Cerqueira,Michel Melo Silva
title: Describing image focused in cognitive and visual details for visually impaired people: An approach to generating inclusive paragraphs
title_zh: 为视障人士描述专注于认知和视觉细节的图像：一种生成包容性段落的方法
comment: Accepted in the 17th International Joint Conference on Computer Vision, Imaging and Computer Graphics Theory and Applications (VISAPP) 2022
comments: Accepted in the 17th International Joint Conference on Computer Vision, Imaging and Computer Graphics Theory and Applications (VISAPP) 2022
abstract: Several services for people with visual disabilities have emerged recently due to achievements in Assistive Technologies and Artificial Intelligence areas. Despite the growth in assistive systems availability, there is a lack of services that support specific tasks, such as understanding the image context presented in online content, e.g., webinars. Image captioning techniques and their variants are limited as Assistive Technologies as they do not match the needs of visually impaired people when generating specific descriptions. We propose an approach for generating context of webinar images combining a dense captioning technique with a set of filters, to fit the captions in our domain, and a language model for the abstractive summary task. The results demonstrated that we can produce descriptions with higher interpretability and focused on the relevant information for that group of people by combining image analysis methods and neural language models.
summary: Several services for people with visual disabilities have emerged recently due to achievements in Assistive Technologies and Artificial Intelligence areas. Despite the growth in assistive systems availability, there is a lack of services that support specific tasks, such as understanding the image context presented in online content, e.g., webinars. Image captioning techniques and their variants are limited as Assistive Technologies as they do not match the needs of visually impaired people when generating specific descriptions. We propose an approach for generating context of webinar images combining a dense captioning technique with a set of filters, to fit the captions in our domain, and a language model for the abstractive summary task. The results demonstrated that we can produce descriptions with higher interpretability and focused on the relevant information for that group of people by combining image analysis methods and neural language models.
abstract_zh: 由于辅助技术和人工智能领域的成就，最近出现了一些针对视障人士的服务。尽管辅助系统的可用性不断增长，但仍缺乏支持特定任务的服务，例如理解在线内容（例如网络研讨会）中呈现的图像上下文。图像字幕技术及其变体作为辅助技术受到限制，因为它们在生成特定描述时不符合视障人士的需求。我们提出了一种生成网络研讨会图像上下文的方法，将密集字幕技术与一组过滤器相结合，以适应我们领域中的字幕，以及用于抽象摘要任务的语言模型。结果表明，通过结合图像分析方法和神经语言模型，我们可以生成具有更高可解释性的描述，并专注于该人群的相关信息。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合密集字幕技术与语言模型的方法，为视障人士生成聚焦认知与视觉细节的图像描述。
AI.motivation: 现有图像字幕技术无法满足视障人士理解在线内容（如网络研讨会）中图像上下文的特定需求。
AI.method: 融合密集字幕技术、领域适配过滤器及神经语言模型，实现图像分析与抽象总结的结合。
AI.result: 生成的描述具有更高可解释性，能精准聚焦视障群体所需的关键信息。
AI.conclusion: 通过整合图像分析与语言模型，可有效生成符合视障人士需求的包容性图像描述。

authors_text: Mingrui Ray Zhang,Ashley Boone,Sara M Behbakht,Alexis Hiniker
title: Understanding the Digital News Consumption Experience During the COVID Pandemic
title_zh: 了解新冠疫情期间的数字新闻消费体验
abstract: During the COVID-19 pandemic, people sought information through digital news platforms. To investigate how to design these platforms to support users' needs in a crisis, we conducted a two-week diary study with 22 participants across the United States. Participants' news-consumption experience followed two stages: in the \textbf{seeking} stage, participants increased their general consumption, motivated by three common informational needs -- specifically, to find, understand and verify relevant news pieces. Participants then moved to the \textbf{sustaining} stage, and coping with the news emotionally became as important as their informational needs. We elicited design ideas from participants and used these to distill six themes for creating digital news platforms that provide better informational and emotional support during a crisis. Thus, we contribute, first, a model of users' needs over time with respect to engaging with crisis news, and second, example design concepts for supporting users' needs in each of these stages.
summary: During the COVID-19 pandemic, people sought information through digital news platforms. To investigate how to design these platforms to support users' needs in a crisis, we conducted a two-week diary study with 22 participants across the United States. Participants' news-consumption experience followed two stages: in the \textbf{seeking} stage, participants increased their general consumption, motivated by three common informational needs -- specifically, to find, understand and verify relevant news pieces. Participants then moved to the \textbf{sustaining} stage, and coping with the news emotionally became as important as their informational needs. We elicited design ideas from participants and used these to distill six themes for creating digital news platforms that provide better informational and emotional support during a crisis. Thus, we contribute, first, a model of users' needs over time with respect to engaging with crisis news, and second, example design concepts for supporting users' needs in each of these stages.
abstract_zh: 在 COVID-19 大流行期间，人们通过数字新闻平台寻求信息。为了调查如何设计这些平台来支持危机中用户的需求，我们对美国各地的 22 名参与者进行了为期两周的日记研究。参与者的新闻消费经历分为两个阶段：在\textbf{寻求}阶段，参与者增加了一般消费，其动机是三种常见的信息需求——具体来说，是寻找、理解和验证相关新闻片段。然后参与者进入\textbf{维持}阶段，在情感上应对新闻变得与他们的信息需求一样重要。我们征求了参与者的设计想法，并利用这些想法提炼出六个主题，以创建数字新闻平台，在危机期间提供更好的信息和情感支持。因此，我们首先提供了一个用户在参与危机新闻方面的需求模型，其次提供了用于支持用户在每个阶段的需求的示例设计概念。

AI.tldr: 研究疫情期间用户数字新闻消费体验，以指导平台设计
AI.motivation: 疫情期间信息需求激增，但现有平台可能无法满足用户的信息和情感需求，需探索用户需求变化以改进设计
AI.method: 采用两周日记研究，跟踪22名美国用户的新闻消费行为，分析其阶段性和需求变化
AI.result: 发现用户经历‘寻求’和‘维持’两个阶段，信息需求转向情感支持，并提出六个设计主题以增强平台的信息与情感支持
AI.conclusion: 提出用户在危机中新闻消费的需求变化模型，并提供分阶段的设计概念以满足用户信息与情感需求

authors_text: Dominic Kao,Rabindra Ratan,Christos Mousas,Amogh Joshi,Edward F. Melcer
title: Audio Matters Too: How Audial Avatar Customization Enhances Visual Avatar Customization
title_zh: 音频也很重要：听觉头像定制如何增强视觉头像定制
abstract: Avatar customization is known to positively affect crucial outcomes in numerous domains. However, it is unknown whether audial customization can confer the same benefits as visual customization. We conducted a preregistered 2 x 2 (visual choice vs. visual assignment x audial choice vs. audial assignment) study in a Java programming game. Participants with visual choice experienced higher avatar identification and autonomy. Participants with audial choice experienced higher avatar identification and autonomy, but only within the group of participants who had visual choice available. Visual choice led to an increase in time spent, and indirectly led to increases in intrinsic motivation, immersion, time spent, future play motivation, and likelihood of game recommendation. Audial choice moderated the majority of these effects. Our results suggest that audial customization plays an important enhancing role vis-à-vis visual customization. However, audial customization appears to have a weaker effect compared to visual customization. We discuss the implications for avatar customization more generally across digital applications.
summary: Avatar customization is known to positively affect crucial outcomes in numerous domains. However, it is unknown whether audial customization can confer the same benefits as visual customization. We conducted a preregistered 2 x 2 (visual choice vs. visual assignment x audial choice vs. audial assignment) study in a Java programming game. Participants with visual choice experienced higher avatar identification and autonomy. Participants with audial choice experienced higher avatar identification and autonomy, but only within the group of participants who had visual choice available. Visual choice led to an increase in time spent, and indirectly led to increases in intrinsic motivation, immersion, time spent, future play motivation, and likelihood of game recommendation. Audial choice moderated the majority of these effects. Our results suggest that audial customization plays an important enhancing role vis-à-vis visual customization. However, audial customization appears to have a weaker effect compared to visual customization. We discuss the implications for avatar customization more generally across digital applications.
abstract_zh: 众所周知，头像定制会对许多领域的关键结果产生积极影响。然而，尚不清楚听觉定制是否能带来与视觉定制相同的好处。我们在 Java 编程游戏中进行了预先注册的 2 x 2（视觉选择与视觉分配 x 听觉选择与听觉分配）研究。具有视觉选择的参与者体验到了更高的化身识别和自主权。具有听觉选择的参与者体验到更高的化身识别和自主权，但仅限于具有视觉选择的参与者组内。视觉选择导致花费时间的增加，并间接导致内在动机、沉浸感、花费时间、未来游戏动机和游戏推荐可能性的增加。听觉选择调节了大部分影响。我们的结果表明，听觉定制相对于视觉定制起着重要的增强作用。然而，与视觉定制相比，听觉定制的效果似乎较弱。我们更广泛地讨论数字应用程序中头像定制的影响。

AI.tldr: 本研究探讨了听觉化身定制（audial avatar customization）是否能像视觉化身定制（visual avatar customization）一样提升用户在数字应用中的体验。
AI.motivation: 尽管视觉化身定制已被证明能提升用户参与度等关键结果，但听觉定制的影响尚不明确。研究旨在填补这一空白，并比较两者的效果差异。
AI.method: 通过预注册的2x2实验设计，在Java编程游戏中测试参与者（视觉选择/分配 × 听觉选择/分配）的组合条件，测量化身认同感、自主性、沉浸感等指标。
AI.result: 视觉选择显著提升化身认同感、自主性、游戏时长及内在动机；听觉选择仅在视觉选择组中产生类似效果。听觉选择增强了多数视觉选择的效应，但整体影响较弱。
AI.conclusion: 听觉定制可增强视觉定制的效果，但作用较弱。研究为数字应用中多模态化身定制的设计提供了理论依据。

result: 视觉选择显著提升化身认同感、自主性、游戏时长及内在动机；听觉选择仅在视觉选择组中产生类似效果。听觉选择增强了多数视觉选择的效应，但整体影响较弱。
conclusion: 听觉定制可增强视觉定制的效果，但作用较弱。研究为数字应用中多模态化身定制的设计提供了理论依据。
search_text: Audio Matters Too: How Audial Avatar Customization Enhances Visual Avatar Customization 音频也很重要：听觉头像定制如何增强视觉头像定制 Avatar customization is known to positively affect crucial outcomes in numerous domains. However, it is unknown whether audial customization can confer the same benefits as visual customization. We conducted a preregistered 2 x 2 (visual choice vs. visual assignment x audial choice vs. audial assignment) study in a Java programming game. Participants with visual choice experienced higher avatar identification and autonomy. Participants with audial choice experienced higher avatar identification and autonomy, but only within the group of participants who had visual choice available. Visual choice led to an increase in time spent, and indirectly led to increases in intrinsic motivation, immersion, time spent, future play motivation, and likelihood of game recommendation. Audial choice moderated the majority of these effects. Our results suggest that audial customization plays an important enhancing role vis-à-vis visual customization. However, audial customization appears to have a weaker effect compared to visual customization. We discuss the implications for avatar customization more generally across digital applications. 众所周知，头像定制会对许多领域的关键结果产生积极影响。然而，尚不清楚听觉定制是否能带来与视觉定制相同的好处。我们在 Java 编程游戏中进行了预先注册的 2 x 2（视觉选择与视觉分配 x 听觉选择与听觉分配）研究。具有视觉选择的参与者体验到了更高的化身识别和自主权。具有听觉选择的参与者体验到更高的化身识别和自主权，但仅限于具有视觉选择的参与者组内。视觉选择导致花费时间的增加，并间接导致内在动机、沉浸感、花费时间、未来游戏动机和游戏推荐可能性的增加。听觉选择调节了大部分影响。我们的结果表明，听觉定制相对于视觉定制起着重要的增强作用。然而，与视觉定制相比，听觉定制的效果似乎较弱。我们更广泛地讨论数字应用程序中头像定制的影响。 本研究探讨了听觉化身定制（audial avatar customization）是否能像视觉化身定制（visual avatar customization）一样提升用户在数字应用中的体验。 Dominic Kao,Rabindra Ratan,Christos Mousas,Amogh Joshi,Edward F. Melcer cs.HC

authors_text: Michael Kläs,Lisa Jöckel,Rasmus Adler,Jan Reich
title: Integrating Testing and Operation-related Quantitative Evidences in Assurance Cases to Argue Safety of Data-Driven AI/ML Components
title_zh: 在保证案例中整合测试和操作相关的定量证据来论证数据驱动的 AI/ML 组件的安全性
abstract: In the future, AI will increasingly find its way into systems that can potentially cause physical harm to humans. For such safety-critical systems, it must be demonstrated that their residual risk does not exceed what is acceptable. This includes, in particular, the AI components that are part of such systems' safety-related functions. Assurance cases are an intensively discussed option today for specifying a sound and comprehensive safety argument to demonstrate a system's safety. In previous work, it has been suggested to argue safety for AI components by structuring assurance cases based on two complementary risk acceptance criteria. One of these criteria is used to derive quantitative targets regarding the AI. The argumentation structures commonly proposed to show the achievement of such quantitative targets, however, focus on failure rates from statistical testing. Further important aspects are only considered in a qualitative manner -- if at all. In contrast, this paper proposes a more holistic argumentation structure for having achieved the target, namely a structure that integrates test results with runtime aspects and the impact of scope compliance and test data quality in a quantitative manner. We elaborate different argumentation options, present the underlying mathematical considerations, and discuss resulting implications for their practical application. Using the proposed argumentation structure might not only increase the integrity of assurance cases but may also allow claims on quantitative targets that would not be justifiable otherwise.
summary: In the future, AI will increasingly find its way into systems that can potentially cause physical harm to humans. For such safety-critical systems, it must be demonstrated that their residual risk does not exceed what is acceptable. This includes, in particular, the AI components that are part of such systems' safety-related functions. Assurance cases are an intensively discussed option today for specifying a sound and comprehensive safety argument to demonstrate a system's safety. In previous work, it has been suggested to argue safety for AI components by structuring assurance cases based on two complementary risk acceptance criteria. One of these criteria is used to derive quantitative targets regarding the AI. The argumentation structures commonly proposed to show the achievement of such quantitative targets, however, focus on failure rates from statistical testing. Further important aspects are only considered in a qualitative manner -- if at all. In contrast, this paper proposes a more holistic argumentation structure for having achieved the target, namely a structure that integrates test results with runtime aspects and the impact of scope compliance and test data quality in a quantitative manner. We elaborate different argumentation options, present the underlying mathematical considerations, and discuss resulting implications for their practical application. Using the proposed argumentation structure might not only increase the integrity of assurance cases but may also allow claims on quantitative targets that would not be justifiable otherwise.
abstract_zh: 未来，人工智能将越来越多地进入可能对人类造成身体伤害的系统。对于此类安全关键系统，必须证明其残余风险不超过可接受的范围。这尤其包括属于此类系统安全相关功能一部分的人工智能组件。保证案例是当今广泛讨论的选项，用于指定合理且全面的安全论证来证明系统的安全性。在之前的工作中，有人建议通过基于两个互补的风险接受标准构建保证案例来论证人工智能组件的安全性。这些标准之一用于得出有关人工智能的定量目标。然而，通常提出的论证结构是为了显示此类定量目标的实现，但重点关注统计测试的失败率。其他重要方面仅以定性方式考虑——如果有的话。相比之下，本文提出了一种更全面的论证结构来实现目标，即以定量的方式将测试结果与运行时方面以及范围合规性和测试数据质量的影响相结合的结构。我们详细阐述了不同的论证选项，提出了潜在的数学考虑，并讨论了其实际应用的结果。使用拟议的论证结构不仅可以提高保证案例的完整性，而且还可以允许对定量目标提出否则不合理的主张。

AI.tldr: 本文提出将测试结果与运行时因素、范围合规性和测试数据质量的定量证据整合到保证案例中，以更全面地论证数据驱动AI/ML组件的安全性。
AI.motivation: 现有保证案例在论证AI组件安全性时，仅依赖统计测试的故障率指标，忽视了运行时因素、测试数据质量等关键定量维度，导致论证完整性不足。
AI.method: 构建融合测试结果与运行时指标的量化论证框架，通过数学建模量化范围合规性与测试数据质量对安全目标的影响，并分析其工程应用可行性。
AI.result: 该方法增强了保证案例的论证完整性，使原本难以量化的安全目标（如长期运行可靠性）具备可验证的数学依据。
AI.conclusion: 通过多维度定量证据整合，可建立更可靠的安全论证体系，为AI/ML组件在安全关键系统的应用提供理论支持。

authors_text: Sayantan Bhadra,Umberto Villa,Mark A. Anastasio
title: Mining the manifolds of deep generative models for multiple data-consistent solutions of ill-posed tomographic imaging problems
title_zh: 挖掘深度生成模型的流形，以实现不适定断层成像问题的多个数据一致的解决方案
comment: Submitted to IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging
comments: Submitted to IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging
abstract: Tomographic imaging is in general an ill-posed inverse problem. Typically, a single regularized image estimate of the sought-after object is obtained from tomographic measurements. However, there may be multiple objects that are all consistent with the same measurement data. The ability to generate such alternate solutions is important because it may enable new assessments of imaging systems. In principle, this can be achieved by means of posterior sampling methods. In recent years, deep neural networks have been employed for posterior sampling with promising results. However, such methods are not yet for use with large-scale tomographic imaging applications. On the other hand, empirical sampling methods may be computationally feasible for large-scale imaging systems and enable uncertainty quantification for practical applications. Empirical sampling involves solving a regularized inverse problem within a stochastic optimization framework to obtain alternate data-consistent solutions. In this work, a new empirical sampling method is proposed that computes multiple solutions of a tomographic inverse problem that are consistent with the same acquired measurement data. The method operates by repeatedly solving an optimization problem in the latent space of a style-based generative adversarial network (StyleGAN), and was inspired by the Photo Upsampling via Latent Space Exploration (PULSE) method that was developed for super-resolution tasks. The proposed method is demonstrated and analyzed via numerical studies that involve two stylized tomographic imaging modalities. These studies establish the ability of the method to perform efficient empirical sampling and uncertainty quantification.
summary: Tomographic imaging is in general an ill-posed inverse problem. Typically, a single regularized image estimate of the sought-after object is obtained from tomographic measurements. However, there may be multiple objects that are all consistent with the same measurement data. The ability to generate such alternate solutions is important because it may enable new assessments of imaging systems. In principle, this can be achieved by means of posterior sampling methods. In recent years, deep neural networks have been employed for posterior sampling with promising results. However, such methods are not yet for use with large-scale tomographic imaging applications. On the other hand, empirical sampling methods may be computationally feasible for large-scale imaging systems and enable uncertainty quantification for practical applications. Empirical sampling involves solving a regularized inverse problem within a stochastic optimization framework to obtain alternate data-consistent solutions. In this work, a new empirical sampling method is proposed that computes multiple solutions of a tomographic inverse problem that are consistent with the same acquired measurement data. The method operates by repeatedly solving an optimization problem in the latent space of a style-based generative adversarial network (StyleGAN), and was inspired by the Photo Upsampling via Latent Space Exploration (PULSE) method that was developed for super-resolution tasks. The proposed method is demonstrated and analyzed via numerical studies that involve two stylized tomographic imaging modalities. These studies establish the ability of the method to perform efficient empirical sampling and uncertainty quantification.
abstract_zh: 断层成像通常是一个不适定的逆问题。通常，从断层摄影测量中获得所需对象的单个正则化图像估计。然而，可能有多个对象都与相同的测量数据一致。生成此类替代解决方案的能力非常重要，因为它可以实现对成像系统的新评估。原则上，这可以通过后验采样方法来实现。近年来，深度神经网络已被用于后验采样，并取得了可喜的结果。然而，此类方法尚未用于大规模层析成像应用。另一方面，经验采样方法对于大规模成像系统在计算上可能是可行的，并且能够对实际应用进行不确定性量化。经验采样涉及在随机优化框架内解决正则化反问题，以获得替代的数据一致解决方案。在这项工作中，提出了一种新的经验采样方法，该方法可以计算与相同获取的测量数据一致的层析反问题的多个解。该方法通过重复解决基于样式的生成对抗网络（StyleGAN）的潜在空间中的优化问题来运行，并且受到为超分辨率任务开发的通过潜在空间探索进行照片上采样（PULSE）方法的启发。通过涉及两种程式化断层扫描成像模式的数值研究来论证和分析所提出的方法。这些研究确立了该方法执行有效经验抽样和不确定性量化的能力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度生成模型的新型经验采样方法，用于生成与同一测量数据一致的多组断层成像解。
AI.motivation: 传统断层成像仅提供单一正则化解，而实际可能存在多个数据一致解。现有后验采样方法难以应用于大规模成像系统，需开发计算可行的替代方案以实现不确定性量化。
AI.method: 在StyleGAN的潜在空间中重复求解优化问题，受PULSE超分辨率方法启发，通过生成对抗网络的潜在空间探索生成多组数据一致解。
AI.result: 数值实验表明该方法在两种断层成像模态中均能有效实现经验采样与不确定性量化，验证了其计算可行性与准确性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为大规模断层成像提供了高效的经验采样框架，有助于提升成像系统评估与不确定性分析能力。

The method operates by repeatedly solving an optimization problem in the latent space of a style-based generative adversarial network (StyleGAN), and was inspired by the Photo Upsampling via Latent Space Exploration (PULSE) method that was developed for super-resolution tasks. The proposed method is demonstrated and analyzed via numerical studies that involve two stylized tomographic imaging modalities. These studies establish the ability of the method to perform efficient empirical sampling and uncertainty quantification. 断层成像通常是一个不适定的逆问题。通常，从断层摄影测量中获得所需对象的单个正则化图像估计。然而，可能有多个对象都与相同的测量数据一致。生成此类替代解决方案的能力非常重要，因为它可以实现对成像系统的新评估。原则上，这可以通过后验采样方法来实现。近年来，深度神经网络已被用于后验采样，并取得了可喜的结果。然而，此类方法尚未用于大规模层析成像应用。另一方面，经验采样方法对于大规模成像系统在计算上可能是可行的，并且能够对实际应用进行不确定性量化。经验采样涉及在随机优化框架内解决正则化反问题，以获得替代的数据一致解决方案。在这项工作中，提出了一种新的经验采样方法，该方法可以计算与相同获取的测量数据一致的层析反问题的多个解。该方法通过重复解决基于样式的生成对抗网络（StyleGAN）的潜在空间中的优化问题来运行，并且受到为超分辨率任务开发的通过潜在空间探索进行照片上采样（PULSE）方法的启发。通过涉及两种程式化断层扫描成像模式的数值研究来论证和分析所提出的方法。这些研究确立了该方法执行有效经验抽样和不确定性量化的能力。 本文提出一种基于深度生成模型的新型经验采样方法，用于生成与同一测量数据一致的多组断层成像解。 Sayantan Bhadra,Umberto Villa,Mark A. Anastasio eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Nan Wu,Stanisław Jastrzębski,Kyunghyun Cho,Krzysztof J. Geras
title: Characterizing and overcoming the greedy nature of learning in multi-modal deep neural networks
title_zh: 表征和克服多模态深度神经网络中学习的贪婪本质
abstract: We hypothesize that due to the greedy nature of learning in multi-modal deep neural networks, these models tend to rely on just one modality while under-fitting the other modalities. Such behavior is counter-intuitive and hurts the models' generalization, as we observe empirically. To estimate the model's dependence on each modality, we compute the gain on the accuracy when the model has access to it in addition to another modality. We refer to this gain as the conditional utilization rate. In the experiments, we consistently observe an imbalance in conditional utilization rates between modalities, across multiple tasks and architectures. Since conditional utilization rate cannot be computed efficiently during training, we introduce a proxy for it based on the pace at which the model learns from each modality, which we refer to as the conditional learning speed. We propose an algorithm to balance the conditional learning speeds between modalities during training and demonstrate that it indeed addresses the issue of greedy learning. The proposed algorithm improves the model's generalization on three datasets: Colored MNIST, ModelNet40, and NVIDIA Dynamic Hand Gesture.
summary: We hypothesize that due to the greedy nature of learning in multi-modal deep neural networks, these models tend to rely on just one modality while under-fitting the other modalities. Such behavior is counter-intuitive and hurts the models' generalization, as we observe empirically. To estimate the model's dependence on each modality, we compute the gain on the accuracy when the model has access to it in addition to another modality. We refer to this gain as the conditional utilization rate. In the experiments, we consistently observe an imbalance in conditional utilization rates between modalities, across multiple tasks and architectures. Since conditional utilization rate cannot be computed efficiently during training, we introduce a proxy for it based on the pace at which the model learns from each modality, which we refer to as the conditional learning speed. We propose an algorithm to balance the conditional learning speeds between modalities during training and demonstrate that it indeed addresses the issue of greedy learning. The proposed algorithm improves the model's generalization on three datasets: Colored MNIST, ModelNet40, and NVIDIA Dynamic Hand Gesture.
abstract_zh: 我们假设，由于多模态深度神经网络学习的贪婪本质，这些模型往往只依赖于一种模态，而对其他模态拟合不足。正如我们根据经验观察到的那样，这种行为是违反直觉的，并且损害了模型的泛化能力。为了估计模型对每种模态的依赖性，我们计算当模型除了另一种模态之外还可以访问它时的准确性增益。我们将此增益称为条件利用率。在实验中，我们一致观察到跨多个任务和架构的模式之间的条件利用率不平衡。由于在训练期间无法有效计算条件利用率，因此我们根据模型从每种模态学习的速度引入了一个代理，我们将其称为条件学习速度。我们提出了一种算法来平衡训练期间模式之间的条件学习速度，并证明它确实解决了贪婪学习的问题。所提出的算法提高了模型在三个数据集上的泛化能力：Colored MNIST、ModelNet40 和 NVIDIA Dynamic Hand Gesture。

AI.tldr: 本文研究多模态深度神经网络中学习的贪婪特性，提出算法解决模型过度依赖单一模态导致的泛化能力下降问题。
AI.motivation: 多模态模型在训练中倾向于依赖某一模态而忽视其他模态，导致欠拟合和泛化能力下降，这一现象影响模型性能。
AI.method: 通过计算条件利用率衡量模态依赖程度，引入条件学习速度作为训练时的代理指标，并设计平衡算法调整不同模态的学习速度。
AI.result: 在Colored MNIST、ModelNet40和NVIDIA Dynamic Hand Gesture三个数据集上，模型泛化能力得到显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 提出的算法有效缓解了多模态模型的贪婪学习问题，通过平衡模态学习速度增强了模型的综合表现。

authors_text: Max W. Shen
title: Trust in AI: Interpretability is not necessary or sufficient, while black-box interaction is necessary and sufficient
title_zh: 对人工智能的信任：可解释性不是必要或充分的，而黑盒交互是必要和充分的
abstract: The problem of human trust in artificial intelligence is one of the most fundamental problems in applied machine learning. Our processes for evaluating AI trustworthiness have substantial ramifications for ML's impact on science, health, and humanity, yet confusion surrounds foundational concepts. What does it mean to trust an AI, and how do humans assess AI trustworthiness? What are the mechanisms for building trustworthy AI? And what is the role of interpretable ML in trust? Here, we draw from statistical learning theory and sociological lenses on human-automation trust to motivate an AI-as-tool framework, which distinguishes human-AI trust from human-AI-human trust. Evaluating an AI's contractual trustworthiness involves predicting future model behavior using behavior certificates (BCs) that aggregate behavioral evidence from diverse sources including empirical out-of-distribution and out-of-task evaluation and theoretical proofs linking model architecture to behavior. We clarify the role of interpretability in trust with a ladder of model access. Interpretability (level 3) is not necessary or even sufficient for trust, while the ability to run a black-box model at-will (level 2) is necessary and sufficient. While interpretability can offer benefits for trust, it can also incur costs. We clarify ways interpretability can contribute to trust, while questioning the perceived centrality of interpretability to trust in popular discourse. How can we empower people with tools to evaluate trust? Instead of trying to understand how a model works, we argue for understanding how a model behaves. Instead of opening up black boxes, we should create more behavior certificates that are more correct, relevant, and understandable. We discuss how to build trusted and trustworthy AI responsibly.
summary: The problem of human trust in artificial intelligence is one of the most fundamental problems in applied machine learning. Our processes for evaluating AI trustworthiness have substantial ramifications for ML's impact on science, health, and humanity, yet confusion surrounds foundational concepts. What does it mean to trust an AI, and how do humans assess AI trustworthiness? What are the mechanisms for building trustworthy AI? And what is the role of interpretable ML in trust? Here, we draw from statistical learning theory and sociological lenses on human-automation trust to motivate an AI-as-tool framework, which distinguishes human-AI trust from human-AI-human trust. Evaluating an AI's contractual trustworthiness involves predicting future model behavior using behavior certificates (BCs) that aggregate behavioral evidence from diverse sources including empirical out-of-distribution and out-of-task evaluation and theoretical proofs linking model architecture to behavior. We clarify the role of interpretability in trust with a ladder of model access. Interpretability (level 3) is not necessary or even sufficient for trust, while the ability to run a black-box model at-will (level 2) is necessary and sufficient. While interpretability can offer benefits for trust, it can also incur costs. We clarify ways interpretability can contribute to trust, while questioning the perceived centrality of interpretability to trust in popular discourse. How can we empower people with tools to evaluate trust? Instead of trying to understand how a model works, we argue for understanding how a model behaves. Instead of opening up black boxes, we should create more behavior certificates that are more correct, relevant, and understandable. We discuss how to build trusted and trustworthy AI responsibly.
abstract_zh: 人类对人工智能的信任问题是应用机器学习中最基本的问题之一。我们评估人工智能可信度的流程对机器学习对科学、健康和人类的影响产生了重大影响，但基本概念仍然存在混乱。信任人工智能意味着什么？人类如何评估人工智能的可信度？构建可信人工智能的机制是什么？可解释的机器学习在信任中扮演什么角色？在这里，我们借鉴统计学习理论和社会学视角来研究人类与自动化的信任，以激发人工智能作为工具的框架，该框架将人类与人工智能的信任与人类与人工智能-人类的信任区分开来。评估人工智能的契约可信度涉及使用行为证书（BC）来预测未来的模型行为，行为证书汇总了来自不同来源的行为证据，包括经验分布外和任务外评估以及将模型架构与行为联系起来的理论证明。我们通过模型访问的阶梯来阐明可解释性在信任中的作用。可解释性（第 3 级）对于信任来说并不是必要的，甚至不是充分的，而随意运行黑盒模型的能力（第 2 级）是必要且充分的。虽然可解释性可以为信任带来好处，但它也会带来成本。我们阐明了可解释性有助于信任的方式，同时质疑可解释性在流行话语中对信任的中心地位。我们如何为人们提供评估信任的工具？我们主张理解模型的行为方式，而不是试图理解模型如何工作。我们不应该打开黑匣子，而应该创建更多更正确、更相关、更容易理解的行为证书。我们讨论如何负责任地构建可信且值得信赖的人工智能。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了人工智能信任问题，提出可解释性并非必要或充分条件，而黑箱交互是必要且充分的，并通过行为证书（BCs）框架重新定义AI可信度评估方法。
AI.motivation: 人类对AI的信任机制存在概念混淆，现有评估方法对AI在科学、健康等领域的应用影响重大，需明确信任本质及构建可信AI的路径。
AI.method: 结合统计学习理论与人机信任社会学视角，提出AI作为工具的框架，通过行为证书整合多源行为证据，并构建模型访问层级梯度分析可解释性作用。
AI.result: 证明可解释性（层级3）非信任必要条件，黑箱模型自主运行能力（层级2）才是必要且充分条件；行为证书能更有效评估模型行为而非依赖可解释性。
AI.conclusion: 应通过行为证书而非单纯可解释性建立AI信任，重点研究模型行为预测与验证，推动可信AI的负责任开发。

We discuss how to build trusted and trustworthy AI responsibly. 人类对人工智能的信任问题是应用机器学习中最基本的问题之一。我们评估人工智能可信度的流程对机器学习对科学、健康和人类的影响产生了重大影响，但基本概念仍然存在混乱。信任人工智能意味着什么？人类如何评估人工智能的可信度？构建可信人工智能的机制是什么？可解释的机器学习在信任中扮演什么角色？在这里，我们借鉴统计学习理论和社会学视角来研究人类与自动化的信任，以激发人工智能作为工具的框架，该框架将人类与人工智能的信任与人类与人工智能-人类的信任区分开来。评估人工智能的契约可信度涉及使用行为证书（BC）来预测未来的模型行为，行为证书汇总了来自不同来源的行为证据，包括经验分布外和任务外评估以及将模型架构与行为联系起来的理论证明。我们通过模型访问的阶梯来阐明可解释性在信任中的作用。可解释性（第 3 级）对于信任来说并不是必要的，甚至不是充分的，而随意运行黑盒模型的能力（第 2 级）是必要且充分的。虽然可解释性可以为信任带来好处，但它也会带来成本。我们阐明了可解释性有助于信任的方式，同时质疑可解释性在流行话语中对信任的中心地位。我们如何为人们提供评估信任的工具？我们主张理解模型的行为方式，而不是试图理解模型如何工作。我们不应该打开黑匣子，而应该创建更多更正确、更相关、更容易理解的行为证书。我们讨论如何负责任地构建可信且值得信赖的人工智能。 本文探讨了人工智能信任问题，提出可解释性并非必要或充分条件，而黑箱交互是必要且充分的，并通过行为证书（BCs）框架重新定义AI可信度评估方法。 Max W. Shen cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Mathias Ibsen,Christian Rathgeb,Pawel Drozdowski,Christoph Busch
title: Face Beneath the Ink: Synthetic Data and Tattoo Removal with Application to Face Recognition
title_zh: 墨水下的脸：合成数据和纹身去除与人脸识别的应用
abstract: Systems that analyse faces have seen significant improvements in recent years and are today used in numerous application scenarios. However, these systems have been found to be negatively affected by facial alterations such as tattoos. To better understand and mitigate the effect of facial tattoos in facial analysis systems, large datasets of images of individuals with and without tattoos are needed. To this end, we propose a generator for automatically adding realistic tattoos to facial images. Moreover, we demonstrate the feasibility of the generation by using a deep learning-based model for removing tattoos from face images. The experimental results show that it is possible to remove facial tattoos from real images without degrading the quality of the image. Additionally, we show that it is possible to improve face recognition accuracy by using the proposed deep learning-based tattoo removal before extracting and comparing facial features.
summary: Systems that analyse faces have seen significant improvements in recent years and are today used in numerous application scenarios. However, these systems have been found to be negatively affected by facial alterations such as tattoos. To better understand and mitigate the effect of facial tattoos in facial analysis systems, large datasets of images of individuals with and without tattoos are needed. To this end, we propose a generator for automatically adding realistic tattoos to facial images. Moreover, we demonstrate the feasibility of the generation by using a deep learning-based model for removing tattoos from face images. The experimental results show that it is possible to remove facial tattoos from real images without degrading the quality of the image. Additionally, we show that it is possible to improve face recognition accuracy by using the proposed deep learning-based tattoo removal before extracting and comparing facial features.
abstract_zh: 近年来，分析人脸的系统已经有了显着的改进，如今已用于许多应用场景。然而，这些系统被发现会受到纹身等面部改变的负面影响。为了更好地理解和减轻面部纹身对面部分析系统的影响，需要有和没有纹身的个体的大型图像数据集。为此，我们提出了一个生成器，用于自动向面部图像添加逼真的纹身。此外，我们通过使用基于深度学习的模型从面部图像中去除纹身来证明生成的可行性。实验结果表明，可以在不降低图像质量的情况下从真实图像中去除面部纹身。此外，我们表明，在提取和比较面部特征之前，使用所提出的基于深度学习的纹身去除可以提高人脸识别的准确性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种生成带纹身人脸合成数据的方法，并利用深度学习模型实现纹身去除，以提升人脸识别准确率。
AI.motivation: 现有面部分析系统受纹身影响较大，缺乏足够数据集且难以有效处理纹身干扰，需改进数据生成与处理技术。
AI.method: 设计自动添加真实纹身的生成器，结合深度学习模型实现纹身去除，并在去除后进行人脸特征提取与比对。
AI.result: 实验表明纹身去除算法可保持图像质量，且预处理后的人脸识别准确率显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为研究纹身对人脸识别的影响提供了有效工具，同时验证了预处理技术对提升系统鲁棒性的价值。

authors_text: Deok-Kyeong Jang,Soomin Park,Sung-Hee Lee
title: Motion Puzzle: Arbitrary Motion Style Transfer by Body Part
title_zh: 运动拼图：通过身体部位进行任意运动风格转移
abstract: This paper presents Motion Puzzle, a novel motion style transfer network that advances the state-of-the-art in several important respects. The Motion Puzzle is the first that can control the motion style of individual body parts, allowing for local style editing and significantly increasing the range of stylized motions. Designed to keep the human's kinematic structure, our framework extracts style features from multiple style motions for different body parts and transfers them locally to the target body parts. Another major advantage is that it can transfer both global and local traits of motion style by integrating the adaptive instance normalization and attention modules while keeping the skeleton topology. Thus, it can capture styles exhibited by dynamic movements, such as flapping and staggering, significantly better than previous work. In addition, our framework allows for arbitrary motion style transfer without datasets with style labeling or motion pairing, making many publicly available motion datasets available for training. Our framework can be easily integrated with motion generation frameworks to create many applications, such as real-time motion transfer. We demonstrate the advantages of our framework with a number of examples and comparisons with previous work.
summary: This paper presents Motion Puzzle, a novel motion style transfer network that advances the state-of-the-art in several important respects. The Motion Puzzle is the first that can control the motion style of individual body parts, allowing for local style editing and significantly increasing the range of stylized motions. Designed to keep the human's kinematic structure, our framework extracts style features from multiple style motions for different body parts and transfers them locally to the target body parts. Another major advantage is that it can transfer both global and local traits of motion style by integrating the adaptive instance normalization and attention modules while keeping the skeleton topology. Thus, it can capture styles exhibited by dynamic movements, such as flapping and staggering, significantly better than previous work. In addition, our framework allows for arbitrary motion style transfer without datasets with style labeling or motion pairing, making many publicly available motion datasets available for training. Our framework can be easily integrated with motion generation frameworks to create many applications, such as real-time motion transfer. We demonstrate the advantages of our framework with a number of examples and comparisons with previous work.
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了 Motion Puzzle，这是一种新颖的运动风格传输网络，它在几个重要方面推进了最先进的技术。 Motion Puzzle 是第一个可以控制各个身体部位的运动风格的游戏，允许进行本地风格编辑并显着增加风格化运动的范围。我们的框架旨在保持人体的运动结构，从不同身体部位的多种风格运动中提取风格特征，并将其本地传输到目标身体部位。另一个主要优点是，它可以通过集成自适应实例归一化和注意力模块来传递运动风格的全局和局部特征，同时保持骨架拓扑。因此，它可以捕捉动态运动所表现出的风格，例如拍打和摇晃，明显优于以前的工作。此外，我们的框架允许任意运动风格传输，无需带有风格标签或运动配对的数据集，从而使许多公开可用的运动数据集可用于训练。我们的框架可以轻松地与运动生成框架集成，以创建许多应用程序，例如实时运动传输。我们通过大量示例以及与之前工作的比较来展示我们框架的优势。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Motion Puzzle网络，实现对人体各部位运动风格的任意迁移，突破了传统方法对整体运动风格的限制。
AI.motivation: 现有运动风格迁移方法难以实现对单个身体部位的局部风格编辑，且依赖标注数据集或运动配对，限制了应用范围。
AI.method: 通过提取不同身体部位的风格特征并局部迁移，结合自适应实例归一化和注意力模块，在保持骨骼拓扑结构的同时实现全局与局部风格迁移，无需依赖标注数据集。
AI.result: 实验表明该框架能更准确捕捉动态运动风格（如挥动、摇晃），并在多个对比实验中优于现有方法，支持实时运动迁移应用。
AI.conclusion: Motion Puzzle通过局部风格控制和数据高效性，在运动风格迁移领域取得突破，为实时应用和生成框架集成提供新思路。

authors_text: Dominik Müller,Iñaki Soto-Rey,Frank Kramer
title: Towards a Guideline for Evaluation Metrics in Medical Image Segmentation
title_zh: 医学图像分割评估指标指南
abstract: In the last decade, research on artificial intelligence has seen rapid growth with deep learning models, especially in the field of medical image segmentation. Various studies demonstrated that these models have powerful prediction capabilities and achieved similar results as clinicians. However, recent studies revealed that the evaluation in image segmentation studies lacks reliable model performance assessment and showed statistical bias by incorrect metric implementation or usage. Thus, this work provides an overview and interpretation guide on the following metrics for medical image segmentation evaluation in binary as well as multi-class problems: Dice similarity coefficient, Jaccard, Sensitivity, Specificity, Rand index, ROC curves, Cohen's Kappa, and Hausdorff distance. As a summary, we propose a guideline for standardized medical image segmentation evaluation to improve evaluation quality, reproducibility, and comparability in the research field.
summary: In the last decade, research on artificial intelligence has seen rapid growth with deep learning models, especially in the field of medical image segmentation. Various studies demonstrated that these models have powerful prediction capabilities and achieved similar results as clinicians. However, recent studies revealed that the evaluation in image segmentation studies lacks reliable model performance assessment and showed statistical bias by incorrect metric implementation or usage. Thus, this work provides an overview and interpretation guide on the following metrics for medical image segmentation evaluation in binary as well as multi-class problems: Dice similarity coefficient, Jaccard, Sensitivity, Specificity, Rand index, ROC curves, Cohen's Kappa, and Hausdorff distance. As a summary, we propose a guideline for standardized medical image segmentation evaluation to improve evaluation quality, reproducibility, and comparability in the research field.
abstract_zh: 在过去的十年中，人工智能的研究随着深度学习模型的发展而快速增长，特别是在医学图像分割领域。各种研究表明，这些模型具有强大的预测能力，并取得了与临床医生相似的结果。然而，最近的研究表明，图像分割研究中的评估缺乏可靠的模型性能评估，并且由于不正确的度量实施或使用而表现出统计偏差。因此，这项工作为二元和多类问题中的医学图像分割评估的以下指标提供了概述和解释指南：Dice 相似系数、Jaccard、灵敏度、特异性、Rand 指数、ROC 曲线、Cohen's Kappa 和 Hausdorff 距离。作为总结，我们提出了标准化医学图像分割评估指南，以提高研究领域的评估质量、可重复性和可比性。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在为医学图像分割的评估指标提供一个指南，以提高评估质量、可重复性和可比性。
AI.motivation: 尽管深度学习模型在医学图像分割领域表现出色，但评估方法存在统计偏差，缺乏可靠的模型性能评估。
AI.method: 本文对多种评估指标进行了概述和解释，包括Dice相似系数、Jaccard指数、灵敏度、特异性、Rand指数、ROC曲线、Cohen's Kappa和Hausdorff距离。
AI.result: 提出了一套标准化的医学图像分割评估指南，以提高评估质量、可重复性和可比性。
AI.conclusion: 标准化评估指南对于提升医学图像分割研究的评估质量和一致性具有重要意义。

search_text: Towards a Guideline for Evaluation Metrics in Medical Image Segmentation 医学图像分割评估指标指南 In the last decade, research on artificial intelligence has seen rapid growth with deep learning models, especially in the field of medical image segmentation. Various studies demonstrated that these models have powerful prediction capabilities and achieved similar results as clinicians. However, recent studies revealed that the evaluation in image segmentation studies lacks reliable model performance assessment and showed statistical bias by incorrect metric implementation or usage. Thus, this work provides an overview and interpretation guide on the following metrics for medical image segmentation evaluation in binary as well as multi-class problems: Dice similarity coefficient, Jaccard, Sensitivity, Specificity, Rand index, ROC curves, Cohen's Kappa, and Hausdorff distance. As a summary, we propose a guideline for standardized medical image segmentation evaluation to improve evaluation quality, reproducibility, and comparability in the research field. 在过去的十年中，人工智能的研究随着深度学习模型的发展而快速增长，特别是在医学图像分割领域。各种研究表明，这些模型具有强大的预测能力，并取得了与临床医生相似的结果。然而，最近的研究表明，图像分割研究中的评估缺乏可靠的模型性能评估，并且由于不正确的度量实施或使用而表现出统计偏差。因此，这项工作为二元和多类问题中的医学图像分割评估的以下指标提供了概述和解释指南：Dice 相似系数、Jaccard、灵敏度、特异性、Rand 指数、ROC 曲线、Cohen's Kappa 和 Hausdorff 距离。作为总结，我们提出了标准化医学图像分割评估指南，以提高研究领域的评估质量、可重复性和可比性。 本文旨在为医学图像分割的评估指标提供一个指南，以提高评估质量、可重复性和可比性。 Dominik Müller,Iñaki Soto-Rey,Frank Kramer eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Suman Samui
title: Single-channel speech enhancement by using psychoacoustical model inspired fusion framework
title_zh: 使用心理声学模型启发的融合框架进行单通道语音增强
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2202.04882
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2202.04882
abstract: When the parameters of Bayesian Short-time Spectral Amplitude (STSA) estimator for speech enhancement are selected based on the characteristics of the human auditory system, the gain function of the estimator becomes more flexible. Although this type of estimator in acoustic domain is quite effective in reducing the back-ground noise at high frequencies, it produces more speech distortions, which make the high-frequency contents of the speech such as friciatives less perceptible in heavy noise conditions, resulting in intelligibility reduction. On the other hand, the speech enhancement scheme, which exploits the psychoacoustic evidence of frequency selectivity in the modulation domain, is found to be able to increase the intelligibility of noisy speech by a substantial amount, but also suffers from the temporal slurring problem due to its essential design constraint. In order to achieve the joint improvements in both the perceived speech quality and intelligibility, we proposed and investigated a fusion framework by combining the merits of acoustic and modulation domain approaches while avoiding their respective weaknesses. Objective measure evaluation shows that the proposed speech enhancement fusion framework can provide consistent improvements in the perceived speech quality and intelligibility across different SNR levels in various noise conditions, while compared to the other baseline techniques.
summary: When the parameters of Bayesian Short-time Spectral Amplitude (STSA) estimator for speech enhancement are selected based on the characteristics of the human auditory system, the gain function of the estimator becomes more flexible. Although this type of estimator in acoustic domain is quite effective in reducing the back-ground noise at high frequencies, it produces more speech distortions, which make the high-frequency contents of the speech such as friciatives less perceptible in heavy noise conditions, resulting in intelligibility reduction. On the other hand, the speech enhancement scheme, which exploits the psychoacoustic evidence of frequency selectivity in the modulation domain, is found to be able to increase the intelligibility of noisy speech by a substantial amount, but also suffers from the temporal slurring problem due to its essential design constraint. In order to achieve the joint improvements in both the perceived speech quality and intelligibility, we proposed and investigated a fusion framework by combining the merits of acoustic and modulation domain approaches while avoiding their respective weaknesses. Objective measure evaluation shows that the proposed speech enhancement fusion framework can provide consistent improvements in the perceived speech quality and intelligibility across different SNR levels in various noise conditions, while compared to the other baseline techniques.
abstract_zh: 当基于人类听觉系统的特性来选择用于语音增强的贝叶斯短时谱幅度（STSA）估计器的参数时，估计器的增益函数变得更加灵活。虽然这种声学域估计器对于降低高频背景噪声相当有效，但它会产生更多的语音失真，使得摩擦音等语音高频内容在强噪声条件下不易被察觉，导致可懂度降低。另一方面，语音增强方案利用了调制域中频率选择性的心理声学证据，被发现能够大幅提高噪声语音的清晰度，但由于其基本的设计限制，也遇到了时间模糊问题。为了实现感知语音质量和清晰度的联合改进，我们提出并研究了一种融合框架，结合了声学和调制域方法的优点，同时避免了各自的缺点。客观测量评估表明，与其他基线技术相比，所提出的语音增强融合框架可以在各种噪声条件下的不同 SNR 水平上提供感知语音质量和清晰度的一致改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种融合声学域和调制域方法的单通道语音增强框架，以提升语音质量和可懂度。
AI.motivation: 现有基于贝叶斯STSA估计器的声学域方法在高频降噪时易导致语音失真，而基于心理声学的调制域方法虽能提升可懂度但存在时域模糊问题，需联合优化两者优势。
AI.method: 设计融合框架结合声学域噪声抑制与调制域心理声学特征选择，通过参数自适应调整实现两域优势互补。
AI.result: 客观评估显示该框架在不同信噪比和噪声环境下均能稳定提升语音感知质量与可懂度，优于基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 融合框架有效解决了单一方法的局限性，在语音增强任务中实现了质量与可懂度的协同提升。

authors_text: Neerav Karani,Georg Brunner,Ertunc Erdil,Simin Fei,Kerem Tezcan,Krishna Chaitanya,Ender Konukoglu
title: A Field of Experts Prior for Adapting Neural Networks at Test Time
title_zh: 在测试时调整神经网络的专家领域
abstract: Performance of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) in image analysis tasks is often marred in the presence of acquisition-related distribution shifts between training and test images. Recently, it has been proposed to tackle this problem by fine-tuning trained CNNs for each test image. Such test-time-adaptation (TTA) is a promising and practical strategy for improving robustness to distribution shifts as it requires neither data sharing between institutions nor annotating additional data. Previous TTA methods use a helper model to increase similarity between outputs and/or features extracted from a test image with those of the training images. Such helpers, which are typically modeled using CNNs, can be task-specific and themselves vulnerable to distribution shifts in their inputs. To overcome these problems, we propose to carry out TTA by matching the feature distributions of test and training images, as modelled by a field-of-experts (FoE) prior. FoEs model complicated probability distributions as products of many simpler expert distributions. We use 1D marginal distributions of a trained task CNN's features as experts in the FoE model. Further, we compute principal components of patches of the task CNN's features, and consider the distributions of PCA loadings as additional experts. We validate the method on 5 MRI segmentation tasks (healthy tissues in 4 anatomical regions and lesions in 1 one anatomy), using data from 17 clinics, and on a MRI registration task, using data from 3 clinics. We find that the proposed FoE-based TTA is generically applicable in multiple tasks, and outperforms all previous TTA methods for lesion segmentation. For healthy tissue segmentation, the proposed method outperforms other task-agnostic methods, but a previous TTA method which is specifically designed for segmentation performs the best for most of the tested datasets. Our code is publicly available.
summary: Performance of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) in image analysis tasks is often marred in the presence of acquisition-related distribution shifts between training and test images. Recently, it has been proposed to tackle this problem by fine-tuning trained CNNs for each test image. Such test-time-adaptation (TTA) is a promising and practical strategy for improving robustness to distribution shifts as it requires neither data sharing between institutions nor annotating additional data. Previous TTA methods use a helper model to increase similarity between outputs and/or features extracted from a test image with those of the training images. Such helpers, which are typically modeled using CNNs, can be task-specific and themselves vulnerable to distribution shifts in their inputs. To overcome these problems, we propose to carry out TTA by matching the feature distributions of test and training images, as modelled by a field-of-experts (FoE) prior. FoEs model complicated probability distributions as products of many simpler expert distributions. We use 1D marginal distributions of a trained task CNN's features as experts in the FoE model. Further, we compute principal components of patches of the task CNN's features, and consider the distributions of PCA loadings as additional experts. We validate the method on 5 MRI segmentation tasks (healthy tissues in 4 anatomical regions and lesions in 1 one anatomy), using data from 17 clinics, and on a MRI registration task, using data from 3 clinics. We find that the proposed FoE-based TTA is generically applicable in multiple tasks, and outperforms all previous TTA methods for lesion segmentation. For healthy tissue segmentation, the proposed method outperforms other task-agnostic methods, but a previous TTA method which is specifically designed for segmentation performs the best for most of the tested datasets. Our code is publicly available.
abstract_zh: 卷积神经网络 (CNN) 在图像分析任务中的性能通常会因训练图像和测试图像之间存在与采集相关的分布变化而受到影响。最近，有人提出通过针对每个测试图像微调经过训练的 CNN 来解决这个问题。这种测试时间适应（TTA）是一种有前途且实用的策略，可以提高分布变化的稳健性，因为它既不需要机构之间共享数据，也不需要注释额外的数据。以前的 TTA 方法使用辅助模型来增加从测试图像提取的输出和/或特征与训练图像的输出和/或特征之间的相似性。此类助手通常使用 CNN 进行建模，可以是特定于任务的，并且本身很容易受到输入分布变化的影响。为了克服这些问题，我们建议通过匹配测试图像和训练图像的特征分布来进行 TTA，如专家领域 (FoE) 先验建模。 FoE 将复杂的概率分布建模为许多更简单的专家分布的乘积。我们使用训练任务 CNN 特征的一维边缘分布作为 FoE 模型中的专家。此外，我们计算任务 CNN 特征补丁的主成分，并将 PCA 负载的分布视为附加专家。我们使用来自 17 个诊所的数据在 5 个 MRI 分割任务（4 个解剖区域中的健康组织和 1 个解剖区域中的病变）上验证该方法，并使用来自 3 个诊所的数据在 MRI 配准任务上验证该方法。我们发现所提出的基于 FoE 的 TTA 一般适用于多个任务，并且优于之前所有用于病变分割的 TTA 方法。对于健康组织分割，所提出的方法优于其他与任务无关的方法，但之前专门为分割设计的 TTA 方法对于大多数测试数据集表现最好。我们的代码是公开的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于场专家（FoE）先验的测试时适应（TTA）方法，通过匹配测试与训练图像的特征分布，提升CNN在分布偏移场景下的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有TTA方法依赖易受分布偏移影响的辅助模型，难以稳定提升模型性能，需探索更通用的特征分布对齐策略。
AI.method: 构建FoE先验模型，将任务CNN特征的1D边缘分布及PCA载荷分布作为专家，通过分布匹配实现跨分布偏移的测试时适应。
AI.result: 在5个MRI分割任务和1个配准任务中，FoE-TTA在病灶分割上优于所有现有方法，健康组织分割中略逊于特定分割优化的TTA方法。
AI.conclusion: FoE-TTA具有跨任务通用性，尤其适合病灶分割，但健康组织分割需结合任务特异性优化以达到最佳效果。

motivation: 现有TTA方法依赖易受分布偏移影响的辅助模型，难以稳定提升模型性能，需探索更通用的特征分布对齐策略。
method: 构建FoE先验模型，将任务CNN特征的1D边缘分布及PCA载荷分布作为专家，通过分布匹配实现跨分布偏移的测试时适应。
result: 在5个MRI分割任务和1个配准任务中，FoE-TTA在病灶分割上优于所有现有方法，健康组织分割中略逊于特定分割优化的TTA方法。
conclusion: FoE-TTA具有跨任务通用性，尤其适合病灶分割，但健康组织分割需结合任务特异性优化以达到最佳效果。
search_text: A Field of Experts Prior for Adapting Neural Networks at Test Time 在测试时调整神经网络的专家领域 Performance of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) in image analysis tasks is often marred in the presence of acquisition-related distribution shifts between training and test images. Recently, it has been proposed to tackle this problem by fine-tuning trained CNNs for each test image. Such test-time-adaptation (TTA) is a promising and practical strategy for improving robustness to distribution shifts as it requires neither data sharing between institutions nor annotating additional data. Previous TTA methods use a helper model to increase similarity between outputs and/or features extracted from a test image with those of the training images. Such helpers, which are typically modeled using CNNs, can be task-specific and themselves vulnerable to distribution shifts in their inputs. To overcome these problems, we propose to carry out TTA by matching the feature distributions of test and training images, as modelled by a field-of-experts (FoE) prior. FoEs model complicated probability distributions as products of many simpler expert distributions. We use 1D marginal distributions of a trained task CNN's features as experts in the FoE model. Further, we compute principal components of patches of the task CNN's features, and consider the distributions of PCA loadings as additional experts. We validate the method on 5 MRI segmentation tasks (healthy tissues in 4 anatomical regions and lesions in 1 one anatomy), using data from 17 clinics, and on a MRI registration task, using data from 3 clinics. We find that the proposed FoE-based TTA is generically applicable in multiple tasks, and outperforms all previous TTA methods for lesion segmentation. For healthy tissue segmentation, the proposed method outperforms other task-agnostic methods, but a previous TTA method which is specifically designed for segmentation performs the best for most of the tested datasets. Our code is publicly available. 卷积神经网络 (CNN) 在图像分析任务中的性能通常会因训练图像和测试图像之间存在与采集相关的分布变化而受到影响。最近，有人提出通过针对每个测试图像微调经过训练的 CNN 来解决这个问题。这种测试时间适应（TTA）是一种有前途且实用的策略，可以提高分布变化的稳健性，因为它既不需要机构之间共享数据，也不需要注释额外的数据。以前的 TTA 方法使用辅助模型来增加从测试图像提取的输出和/或特征与训练图像的输出和/或特征之间的相似性。此类助手通常使用 CNN 进行建模，可以是特定于任务的，并且本身很容易受到输入分布变化的影响。为了克服这些问题，我们建议通过匹配测试图像和训练图像的特征分布来进行 TTA，如专家领域 (FoE) 先验建模。 FoE 将复杂的概率分布建模为许多更简单的专家分布的乘积。我们使用训练任务 CNN 特征的一维边缘分布作为 FoE 模型中的专家。此外，我们计算任务 CNN 特征补丁的主成分，并将 PCA 负载的分布视为附加专家。我们使用来自 17 个诊所的数据在 5 个 MRI 分割任务（4 个解剖区域中的健康组织和 1 个解剖区域中的病变）上验证该方法，并使用来自 3 个诊所的数据在 MRI 配准任务上验证该方法。我们发现所提出的基于 FoE 的 TTA 一般适用于多个任务，并且优于之前所有用于病变分割的 TTA 方法。对于健康组织分割，所提出的方法优于其他与任务无关的方法，但之前专门为分割设计的 TTA 方法对于大多数测试数据集表现最好。我们的代码是公开的。 本文提出一种基于场专家（FoE）先验的测试时适应（TTA）方法，通过匹配测试与训练图像的特征分布，提升CNN在分布偏移场景下的鲁棒性。 Neerav Karani,Georg Brunner,Ertunc Erdil,Simin Fei,Kerem Tezcan,Krishna Chaitanya,Ender Konukoglu cs.CV,eess.IV,stat.ML

authors_text: Stefano D'Aronco,Giorgio Trumpy,David Pfluger,Jan Dirk Wegner
title: A Deep Learning Approach for Digital Color Reconstruction of Lenticular Films
title_zh: 用于光栅胶片数字色彩重建的深度学习方法
abstract: We propose the first accurate digitization and color reconstruction process for historical lenticular film that is robust to artifacts. Lenticular films emerged in the 1920s and were one of the first technologies that permitted to capture full color information in motion. The technology leverages an RGB filter and cylindrical lenticules embossed on the film surface to encode the color in the horizontal spatial dimension of the image. To project the pictures the encoding process was reversed using an appropriate analog device. In this work, we introduce an automated, fully digital pipeline to process the scan of lenticular films and colorize the image. Our method merges deep learning with a model-based approach in order to maximize the performance while making sure that the reconstructed colored images truthfully match the encoded color information. Our model employs different strategies to achieve an effective color reconstruction, in particular (i) we use data augmentation to create a robust lenticule segmentation network, (ii) we fit the lenticules raster prediction to obtain a precise vectorial lenticule localization, and (iii) we train a colorization network that predicts interpolation coefficients in order to obtain a truthful colorization. We validate the proposed method on a lenticular film dataset and compare it to other approaches. Since no colored groundtruth is available as reference, we conduct a user study to validate our method in a subjective manner. The results of the study show that the proposed method is largely preferred with respect to other existing and baseline methods.
summary: We propose the first accurate digitization and color reconstruction process for historical lenticular film that is robust to artifacts. Lenticular films emerged in the 1920s and were one of the first technologies that permitted to capture full color information in motion. The technology leverages an RGB filter and cylindrical lenticules embossed on the film surface to encode the color in the horizontal spatial dimension of the image. To project the pictures the encoding process was reversed using an appropriate analog device. In this work, we introduce an automated, fully digital pipeline to process the scan of lenticular films and colorize the image. Our method merges deep learning with a model-based approach in order to maximize the performance while making sure that the reconstructed colored images truthfully match the encoded color information. Our model employs different strategies to achieve an effective color reconstruction, in particular (i) we use data augmentation to create a robust lenticule segmentation network, (ii) we fit the lenticules raster prediction to obtain a precise vectorial lenticule localization, and (iii) we train a colorization network that predicts interpolation coefficients in order to obtain a truthful colorization. We validate the proposed method on a lenticular film dataset and compare it to other approaches. Since no colored groundtruth is available as reference, we conduct a user study to validate our method in a subjective manner. The results of the study show that the proposed method is largely preferred with respect to other existing and baseline methods.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了第一个针对历史光栅胶片的精确数字化和色彩重建过程，该过程对伪影具有鲁棒性。光栅胶片出现于 20 年代，是最早能够捕捉运动中的全彩色信息的技术之一。该技术利用 RGB 滤镜和压印在胶片表面的圆柱形微透镜，对图像的水平空间维度中的颜色进行编码。为了投影图像，使用适当的模拟设备反转编码过程。在这项工作中，我们引入了一种自动化的全数字管道来处理光栅胶片的扫描并对图像进行着色。我们的方法将深度学习与基于模型的方法相结合，以最大限度地提高性能，同时确保重建的彩色图像与编码的颜色信息真实匹配。我们的模型采用不同的策略来实现有效的颜色重建，特别是（i）我们使用数据增强来创建强大的微透镜分割网络，（ii）我们拟合微透镜光栅预测以获得精确的矢量微透镜定位，以及（iii）我们训练一个预测插值系数的着色网络以获得真实的着色。我们在光栅胶片数据集上验证了所提出的方法，并将其与其他方法进行比较。由于没有彩色真实数据可供参考，我们进行了用户研究以主观方式验证我们的方法。研究结果表明，相对于其他现有方法和基线方法，所提出的方法在很大程度上是首选的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度学习的数字方法，用于准确重建历史棱镜胶片的色彩信息。
AI.motivation: 棱镜胶片作为早期动态彩色影像技术，因传统模拟设备难以处理且存在伪影，亟需一种自动化、高精度的数字化解决方案。
AI.method: 结合深度学习与模型驱动方法，通过数据增强实现棱镜分割、栅格预测定位棱镜位置，并训练色彩化网络预测插值系数以还原真实色彩。
AI.result: 在用户主观测试中，该方法显著优于现有技术，尽管缺乏彩色真值数据，但用户更认可其重建效果。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法有效实现了历史棱镜胶片的高保真色彩重建，为文化遗产数字化提供了可行方案。

search_text: A Deep Learning Approach for Digital Color Reconstruction of Lenticular Films 用于光栅胶片数字色彩重建的深度学习方法 We propose the first accurate digitization and color reconstruction process for historical lenticular film that is robust to artifacts. Lenticular films emerged in the 1920s and were one of the first technologies that permitted to capture full color information in motion. The technology leverages an RGB filter and cylindrical lenticules embossed on the film surface to encode the color in the horizontal spatial dimension of the image. To project the pictures the encoding process was reversed using an appropriate analog device. In this work, we introduce an automated, fully digital pipeline to process the scan of lenticular films and colorize the image. Our method merges deep learning with a model-based approach in order to maximize the performance while making sure that the reconstructed colored images truthfully match the encoded color information. Our model employs different strategies to achieve an effective color reconstruction, in particular (i) we use data augmentation to create a robust lenticule segmentation network, (ii) we fit the lenticules raster prediction to obtain a precise vectorial lenticule localization, and (iii) we train a colorization network that predicts interpolation coefficients in order to obtain a truthful colorization. We validate the proposed method on a lenticular film dataset and compare it to other approaches. Since no colored groundtruth is available as reference, we conduct a user study to validate our method in a subjective manner. The results of the study show that the proposed method is largely preferred with respect to other existing and baseline methods. 我们提出了第一个针对历史光栅胶片的精确数字化和色彩重建过程，该过程对伪影具有鲁棒性。光栅胶片出现于 20 年代，是最早能够捕捉运动中的全彩色信息的技术之一。该技术利用 RGB 滤镜和压印在胶片表面的圆柱形微透镜，对图像的水平空间维度中的颜色进行编码。为了投影图像，使用适当的模拟设备反转编码过程。在这项工作中，我们引入了一种自动化的全数字管道来处理光栅胶片的扫描并对图像进行着色。我们的方法将深度学习与基于模型的方法相结合，以最大限度地提高性能，同时确保重建的彩色图像与编码的颜色信息真实匹配。我们的模型采用不同的策略来实现有效的颜色重建，特别是（i）我们使用数据增强来创建强大的微透镜分割网络，（ii）我们拟合微透镜光栅预测以获得精确的矢量微透镜定位，以及（iii）我们训练一个预测插值系数的着色网络以获得真实的着色。我们在光栅胶片数据集上验证了所提出的方法，并将其与其他方法进行比较。由于没有彩色真实数据可供参考，我们进行了用户研究以主观方式验证我们的方法。研究结果表明，相对于其他现有方法和基线方法，所提出的方法在很大程度上是首选的。 本文提出了一种基于深度学习的数字方法，用于准确重建历史棱镜胶片的色彩信息。 Stefano D'Aronco,Giorgio Trumpy,David Pfluger,Jan Dirk Wegner eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Ketan Fatania,Carolin M. Pirkl,Marion I. Menzel,Peter Hall,Mohammad Golbabaee
title: A Plug-and-Play Approach to Multiparametric Quantitative MRI: Image Reconstruction using Pre-Trained Deep Denoisers
title_zh: 多参数定量 MRI 的即插即用方法：使用预先训练的深度降噪器进行图像重建
abstract: Current spatiotemporal deep learning approaches to Magnetic Resonance Fingerprinting (MRF) build artefact-removal models customised to a particular k-space subsampling pattern which is used for fast (compressed) acquisition. This may not be useful when the acquisition process is unknown during training of the deep learning model and/or changes during testing time. This paper proposes an iterative deep learning plug-and-play reconstruction approach to MRF which is adaptive to the forward acquisition process. Spatiotemporal image priors are learned by an image denoiser i.e. a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), trained to remove generic white gaussian noise (not a particular subsampling artefact) from data. This CNN denoiser is then used as a data-driven shrinkage operator within the iterative reconstruction algorithm. This algorithm with the same denoiser model is then tested on two simulated acquisition processes with distinct subsampling patterns. The results show consistent de-aliasing performance against both acquisition schemes and accurate mapping of tissues' quantitative bio-properties. Software available: https://github.com/ketanfatania/QMRI-PnP-Recon-POC
summary: Current spatiotemporal deep learning approaches to Magnetic Resonance Fingerprinting (MRF) build artefact-removal models customised to a particular k-space subsampling pattern which is used for fast (compressed) acquisition. This may not be useful when the acquisition process is unknown during training of the deep learning model and/or changes during testing time. This paper proposes an iterative deep learning plug-and-play reconstruction approach to MRF which is adaptive to the forward acquisition process. Spatiotemporal image priors are learned by an image denoiser i.e. a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), trained to remove generic white gaussian noise (not a particular subsampling artefact) from data. This CNN denoiser is then used as a data-driven shrinkage operator within the iterative reconstruction algorithm. This algorithm with the same denoiser model is then tested on two simulated acquisition processes with distinct subsampling patterns. The results show consistent de-aliasing performance against both acquisition schemes and accurate mapping of tissues' quantitative bio-properties. Software available: https://github.com/ketanfatania/QMRI-PnP-Recon-POC
abstract_zh: 当前磁共振指纹 (MRF) 的时空深度学习方法构建了针对特定 k 空间子采样模式定制的伪影去除模型，用于快速（压缩）采集。当深度学习模型训练期间采集过程未知和/或测试期间发生变化时，这可能没有用。本文提出了一种适应前向采集过程的迭代深度学习即插即用 MRF 重建方法。时空图像先验是通过图像降噪器（即卷积神经网络 (CNN)）学习的，经过训练可以从数据中去除通用白高斯噪声（不是特定的子采样伪影）。然后，该 CNN 降噪器在迭代重建算法中用作数据驱动的收缩算子。然后在具有不同子采样模式的两个模拟采集过程中测试具有相同降噪器模型的算法。结果显示，两种采集方案均具有一致的去锯齿性能，并且能够准确映射组织的定量生物特性。可用软件：https://github.com/ketanfatania/QMRI-PnP-Recon-POC

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种即插即用的深度学习方法，用于磁共振指纹成像（MRF）的图像重建，通过预训练的深度去噪器适应不同的采集过程。
AI.motivation: 现有MRF方法依赖特定k空间欠采样模式训练的模型，难以应对训练时未知或测试时变化的采集过程，限制了实际应用灵活性。
AI.method: 利用预训练卷积神经网络（CNN）作为通用去噪器，将其作为数据驱动的收缩算子嵌入迭代重建算法，实现对不同欠采样模式的自适应重建。
AI.result: 在两种不同模拟采集方案下均取得稳定去混叠效果，并准确映射组织定量生物参数，验证了方法的鲁棒性与有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过解耦采集过程与重建模型，为动态或未知采样场景下的定量MRI重建提供了通用解决方案。

search_text: A Plug-and-Play Approach to Multiparametric Quantitative MRI: Image Reconstruction using Pre-Trained Deep Denoisers 多参数定量 MRI 的即插即用方法：使用预先训练的深度降噪器进行图像重建 Current spatiotemporal deep learning approaches to Magnetic Resonance Fingerprinting (MRF) build artefact-removal models customised to a particular k-space subsampling pattern which is used for fast (compressed) acquisition. This may not be useful when the acquisition process is unknown during training of the deep learning model and/or changes during testing time. This paper proposes an iterative deep learning plug-and-play reconstruction approach to MRF which is adaptive to the forward acquisition process. Spatiotemporal image priors are learned by an image denoiser i.e. a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), trained to remove generic white gaussian noise (not a particular subsampling artefact) from data. This CNN denoiser is then used as a data-driven shrinkage operator within the iterative reconstruction algorithm. This algorithm with the same denoiser model is then tested on two simulated acquisition processes with distinct subsampling patterns. The results show consistent de-aliasing performance against both acquisition schemes and accurate mapping of tissues' quantitative bio-properties. Software available: https://github.com/ketanfatania/QMRI-PnP-Recon-POC 当前磁共振指纹 (MRF) 的时空深度学习方法构建了针对特定 k 空间子采样模式定制的伪影去除模型，用于快速（压缩）采集。当深度学习模型训练期间采集过程未知和/或测试期间发生变化时，这可能没有用。本文提出了一种适应前向采集过程的迭代深度学习即插即用 MRF 重建方法。时空图像先验是通过图像降噪器（即卷积神经网络 (CNN)）学习的，经过训练可以从数据中去除通用白高斯噪声（不是特定的子采样伪影）。然后，该 CNN 降噪器在迭代重建算法中用作数据驱动的收缩算子。然后在具有不同子采样模式的两个模拟采集过程中测试具有相同降噪器模型的算法。结果显示，两种采集方案均具有一致的去锯齿性能，并且能够准确映射组织的定量生物特性。可用软件：https://github.com/ketanfatania/QMRI-PnP-Recon-POC 本文提出一种即插即用的深度学习方法，用于磁共振指纹成像（MRF）的图像重建，通过预训练的深度去噪器适应不同的采集过程。 Ketan Fatania,Carolin M. Pirkl,Marion I. Menzel,Peter Hall,Mohammad Golbabaee eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Haozhe Jia,Chao Bai,Weidong Cai,Heng Huang,Yong Xia
title: HNF-Netv2 for Brain Tumor Segmentation using multi-modal MR Imaging
title_zh: 使用多模态 MR 成像进行脑肿瘤分割的 HNF-Netv2
comment: RSNA 2021 Brain Tumor AI Challenge Top Solution. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2012.15318
comments: RSNA 2021 Brain Tumor AI Challenge Top Solution. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2012.15318
abstract: In our previous work, $i.e.$, HNF-Net, high-resolution feature representation and light-weight non-local self-attention mechanism are exploited for brain tumor segmentation using multi-modal MR imaging. In this paper, we extend our HNF-Net to HNF-Netv2 by adding inter-scale and intra-scale semantic discrimination enhancing blocks to further exploit global semantic discrimination for the obtained high-resolution features. We trained and evaluated our HNF-Netv2 on the multi-modal Brain Tumor Segmentation Challenge (BraTS) 2021 dataset. The result on the test set shows that our HNF-Netv2 achieved the average Dice scores of 0.878514, 0.872985, and 0.924919, as well as the Hausdorff distances ($95\%$) of 8.9184, 16.2530, and 4.4895 for the enhancing tumor, tumor core, and whole tumor, respectively. Our method won the RSNA 2021 Brain Tumor AI Challenge Prize (Segmentation Task), which ranks 8th out of all 1250 submitted results.
summary: In our previous work, $i.e.$, HNF-Net, high-resolution feature representation and light-weight non-local self-attention mechanism are exploited for brain tumor segmentation using multi-modal MR imaging. In this paper, we extend our HNF-Net to HNF-Netv2 by adding inter-scale and intra-scale semantic discrimination enhancing blocks to further exploit global semantic discrimination for the obtained high-resolution features. We trained and evaluated our HNF-Netv2 on the multi-modal Brain Tumor Segmentation Challenge (BraTS) 2021 dataset. The result on the test set shows that our HNF-Netv2 achieved the average Dice scores of 0.878514, 0.872985, and 0.924919, as well as the Hausdorff distances ($95\%$) of 8.9184, 16.2530, and 4.4895 for the enhancing tumor, tumor core, and whole tumor, respectively. Our method won the RSNA 2021 Brain Tumor AI Challenge Prize (Segmentation Task), which ranks 8th out of all 1250 submitted results.
abstract_zh: 在我们之前的工作中，利用 HNF-Net、高分辨率特征表示和轻量级非局部自注意力机制，利用多模态 MR 成像进行脑肿瘤分割。在本文中，我们通过添加尺度间和尺度内语义辨别增强块将 HNF-Net 扩展到 HNF-Netv2，以进一步利用所获得的高分辨率特征的全局语义辨别。我们在多模式脑肿瘤分割挑战 (BraTS) 2021 数据集上训练和评估了我们的 HNF-Netv2。测试集上的结果表明，我们的 HNF-Netv2 的平均 Dice 得分为 0.878514、0.872985 和 0.924919，增强肿瘤、肿瘤核心和整个肿瘤的 Hausdorff 距离（$95\%$）分别为 8.9184、16.2530 和 4.4895。我们的方法赢得了 RSNA 2021 脑肿瘤 AI 挑战奖（分割任务），在所有 1250 个提交的结果中排名第 8。

AI.tldr: 本文提出HNF-Netv2网络，通过多模态MRI影像进行脑肿瘤分割，改进了之前的HNF-Net模型。
AI.motivation: 为提升脑肿瘤分割精度，通过引入跨尺度和同尺度语义判别模块增强高分辨率特征的全局语义判别能力。
AI.method: 在HNF-Net基础上添加跨尺度与同尺度语义判别增强块，利用多模态MRI数据训练模型并参与RSNA 2021挑战赛。
AI.result: 在BraTS 2021测试集上，Dice分数分别为0.8785、0.8730、0.9249，Hausdorff距离为8.92、16.25、4.49，获RSNA挑战赛分割任务第8名。
AI.conclusion: HNF-Netv2通过增强语义判别能力显著提升了脑肿瘤分割性能，验证了方法的有效性和竞争力。

authors_text: David E. J. Waddington,Nicholas Hindley,Neha Koonjoo,Christopher Chiu,Tess Reynolds,Paul Z. Y. Liu,Bo Zhu,Danyal Bhutto,Chiara Paganelli,Paul J. Keall,Matthew S. Rosen
title: On Real-time Image Reconstruction with Neural Networks for MRI-guided Radiotherapy
title_zh: MRI引导放射治疗中神经网络实时图像重建
comment: 12 pages, 6 figures, 1 table. v2 has a typo in eqn 1 corrected and references added to the discussion
comments: 12 pages, 6 figures, 1 table. v2 has a typo in eqn 1 corrected and references added to the discussion
abstract: MRI-guidance techniques that dynamically adapt radiation beams to follow tumor motion in real-time will lead to more accurate cancer treatments and reduced collateral healthy tissue damage. The gold-standard for reconstruction of undersampled MR data is compressed sensing (CS) which is computationally slow and limits the rate that images can be available for real-time adaptation. Here, we demonstrate the use of automated transform by manifold approximation (AUTOMAP), a generalized framework that maps raw MR signal to the target image domain, to rapidly reconstruct images from undersampled radial k-space data. The AUTOMAP neural network was trained to reconstruct images from a golden-angle radial acquisition, a benchmark for motion-sensitive imaging, on lung cancer patient data and generic images from ImageNet. Model training was subsequently augmented with motion-encoded k-space data derived from videos in the YouTube-8M dataset to encourage motion robust reconstruction. We find that AUTOMAP-reconstructed radial k-space has equivalent accuracy to CS but with much shorter processing times after initial fine-tuning on retrospectively acquired lung cancer patient data. Validation of motion-trained models with a virtual dynamic lung tumor phantom showed that the generalized motion properties learned from YouTube lead to improved target tracking accuracy. Our work shows that AUTOMAP can achieve real-time, accurate reconstruction of radial data. These findings imply that neural-network-based reconstruction is potentially superior to existing approaches for real-time image guidance applications.

The gold-standard for reconstruction of undersampled MR data is compressed sensing (CS) which is computationally slow and limits the rate that images can be available for real-time adaptation. Here, we demonstrate the use of automated transform by manifold approximation (AUTOMAP), a generalized framework that maps raw MR signal to the target image domain, to rapidly reconstruct images from undersampled radial k-space data. The AUTOMAP neural network was trained to reconstruct images from a golden-angle radial acquisition, a benchmark for motion-sensitive imaging, on lung cancer patient data and generic images from ImageNet. Model training was subsequently augmented with motion-encoded k-space data derived from videos in the YouTube-8M dataset to encourage motion robust reconstruction. We find that AUTOMAP-reconstructed radial k-space has equivalent accuracy to CS but with much shorter processing times after initial fine-tuning on retrospectively acquired lung cancer patient data. Our work shows that AUTOMAP can achieve real-time, accurate reconstruction of radial data. These findings imply that neural-network-based reconstruction is potentially superior to existing approaches for real-time image guidance applications.
abstract_zh: 动态调整辐射束以实时跟踪肿瘤运动的 MRI 引导技术将带来更准确的癌症治疗并减少附带的健康组织损伤。重建欠采样 MR 数据的黄金标准是压缩感知 (CS)，它的计算速度较慢，并且限制了图像可用于实时适应的速率。在这里，我们演示了如何使用流形近似自动变换 (AUTOMAP)，这是一种将原始 MR 信号映射到目标图像域的通用框架，可以从欠采样径向 k 空间数据快速重建图像。 AUTOMAP 神经网络经过训练，可以根据肺癌患者数据和 ImageNet 的通用图像重建黄金角径向采集图像，这是运动敏感成像的基准。随后使用从 YouTube-8M 数据集中的视频派生的运动编码 k 空间数据来增强模型训练，以鼓励运动鲁棒重建。我们发现，AUTOMAP 重建的径向 k 空间具有与 CS 相当的精度，但在对回顾性获取的肺癌患者数据进行初步微调后，处理时间要短得多。使用虚拟动态肺肿瘤模型对运动训练模型进行的验证表明，从 YouTube 学习到的广义运动特性可以提高目标跟踪的准确性。我们的工作表明AUTOMAP可以实现径向数据的实时、准确重建。这些发现意味着基于神经网络的重建可能优于实时图像引导应用的现有方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于神经网络的实时MRI图像重建方法，用于MRI引导的放射治疗。
AI.motivation: 传统压缩感知（CS）方法在处理欠采样MRI数据时计算速度慢，难以满足实时肿瘤追踪的需要，亟需更高效的重建技术。
AI.method: 采用自动生成变换流形逼近框架（AUTOMAP）训练神经网络，利用肺部癌症患者数据和ImageNet图像进行模型训练，并通过YouTube-8M数据集的运动编码k空间数据增强模型的运动鲁棒性。
AI.result: AUTOMAP重建的径向k空间数据在精度上与CS相当，但处理速度显著提升；运动训练模型在虚拟动态肺部肿瘤实验中表现出更优的目标追踪精度。
AI.conclusion: 基于神经网络的重建方法（如AUTOMAP）在实时MRI引导应用中优于传统方法，可提升放射治疗的准确性与效率。

Our work shows that AUTOMAP can achieve real-time, accurate reconstruction of radial data. These findings imply that neural-network-based reconstruction is potentially superior to existing approaches for real-time image guidance applications. 动态调整辐射束以实时跟踪肿瘤运动的 MRI 引导技术将带来更准确的癌症治疗并减少附带的健康组织损伤。重建欠采样 MR 数据的黄金标准是压缩感知 (CS)，它的计算速度较慢，并且限制了图像可用于实时适应的速率。在这里，我们演示了如何使用流形近似自动变换 (AUTOMAP)，这是一种将原始 MR 信号映射到目标图像域的通用框架，可以从欠采样径向 k 空间数据快速重建图像。 AUTOMAP 神经网络经过训练，可以根据肺癌患者数据和 ImageNet 的通用图像重建黄金角径向采集图像，这是运动敏感成像的基准。随后使用从 YouTube-8M 数据集中的视频派生的运动编码 k 空间数据来增强模型训练，以鼓励运动鲁棒重建。我们发现，AUTOMAP 重建的径向 k 空间具有与 CS 相当的精度，但在对回顾性获取的肺癌患者数据进行初步微调后，处理时间要短得多。使用虚拟动态肺肿瘤模型对运动训练模型进行的验证表明，从 YouTube 学习到的广义运动特性可以提高目标跟踪的准确性。我们的工作表明AUTOMAP可以实现径向数据的实时、准确重建。这些发现意味着基于神经网络的重建可能优于实时图像引导应用的现有方法。 本文提出了一种基于神经网络的实时MRI图像重建方法，用于MRI引导的放射治疗。 David E. J. Waddington,Nicholas Hindley,Neha Koonjoo,Christopher Chiu,Tess Reynolds,Paul Z. Y. Liu,Bo Zhu,Danyal Bhutto,Chiara Paganelli,Paul J. Keall,Matthew S. Rosen physics.med-ph,cs.CV,eess.IV

authors_text: Anastasios N Angelopoulos,Amit P Kohli,Stephen Bates,Michael I Jordan,Jitendra Malik,Thayer Alshaabi,Srigokul Upadhyayula,Yaniv Romano
title: Image-to-Image Regression with Distribution-Free Uncertainty Quantification and Applications in Imaging
title_zh: 无分布不确定性量化的图像到图像回归及其在成像中的应用
abstract: Image-to-image regression is an important learning task, used frequently in biological imaging. Current algorithms, however, do not generally offer statistical guarantees that protect against a model's mistakes and hallucinations. To address this, we develop uncertainty quantification techniques with rigorous statistical guarantees for image-to-image regression problems. In particular, we show how to derive uncertainty intervals around each pixel that are guaranteed to contain the true value with a user-specified confidence probability. Our methods work in conjunction with any base machine learning model, such as a neural network, and endow it with formal mathematical guarantees -- regardless of the true unknown data distribution or choice of model. Furthermore, they are simple to implement and computationally inexpensive. We evaluate our procedure on three image-to-image regression tasks: quantitative phase microscopy, accelerated magnetic resonance imaging, and super-resolution transmission electron microscopy of a Drosophila melanogaster brain.
summary: Image-to-image regression is an important learning task, used frequently in biological imaging. Current algorithms, however, do not generally offer statistical guarantees that protect against a model's mistakes and hallucinations. To address this, we develop uncertainty quantification techniques with rigorous statistical guarantees for image-to-image regression problems. In particular, we show how to derive uncertainty intervals around each pixel that are guaranteed to contain the true value with a user-specified confidence probability. Our methods work in conjunction with any base machine learning model, such as a neural network, and endow it with formal mathematical guarantees -- regardless of the true unknown data distribution or choice of model. Furthermore, they are simple to implement and computationally inexpensive. We evaluate our procedure on three image-to-image regression tasks: quantitative phase microscopy, accelerated magnetic resonance imaging, and super-resolution transmission electron microscopy of a Drosophila melanogaster brain.
abstract_zh: 图像到图像回归是一项重要的学习任务，在生物成像中经常使用。然而，当前的算法通常不提供统计保证来防止模型的错误和幻觉。为了解决这个问题，我们开发了不确定性量化技术，为图像到图像回归问题提供严格的统计保证。特别是，我们展示了如何导出每个像素周围的不确定区间，这些不确定区间保证包含具有用户指定的置信概率的真实值。我们的方法与任何基础机器学习模型（例如神经网络）结合使用，并赋予其形式数学保证——无论真正的未知数据分布或模型选择如何。此外，它们易于实现且计算成本低廉。我们在三个图像到图像回归任务上评估我们的程序：定量相位显微镜、加速磁共振成像和果蝇大脑的超分辨率透射电子显微镜。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需假设数据分布的图像到图像回归不确定性量化方法，并应用于生物成像领域。
AI.motivation: 现有图像到图像回归算法缺乏统计保证，无法有效防范模型错误和幻觉，需开发具有严格数学保证的不确定性量化技术。
AI.method: 通过为每个像素生成置信区间，确保真实值包含概率达用户指定水平，该方法兼容任意基础模型（如神经网络），且计算成本低。
AI.result: 在定量相位显微镜、加速磁共振成像和果蝇大脑超分辨率电子显微镜任务中验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为图像回归提供了形式化保证，具有通用性、易实现性和计算高效性，适用于多种生物成像场景。

authors_text: Matthew Tancik,Vincent Casser,Xinchen Yan,Sabeek Pradhan,Ben Mildenhall,Pratul P. Srinivasan,Jonathan T. Barron,Henrik Kretzschmar
title: Block-NeRF: Scalable Large Scene Neural View Synthesis
title_zh: Block-NeRF：可扩展的大场景神经视图合成
abstract: We present Block-NeRF, a variant of Neural Radiance Fields that can represent large-scale environments. Specifically, we demonstrate that when scaling NeRF to render city-scale scenes spanning multiple blocks, it is vital to decompose the scene into individually trained NeRFs. This decomposition decouples rendering time from scene size, enables rendering to scale to arbitrarily large environments, and allows per-block updates of the environment. We adopt several architectural changes to make NeRF robust to data captured over months under different environmental conditions. We add appearance embeddings, learned pose refinement, and controllable exposure to each individual NeRF, and introduce a procedure for aligning appearance between adjacent NeRFs so that they can be seamlessly combined. We build a grid of Block-NeRFs from 2.8 million images to create the largest neural scene representation to date, capable of rendering an entire neighborhood of San Francisco.
summary: We present Block-NeRF, a variant of Neural Radiance Fields that can represent large-scale environments. Specifically, we demonstrate that when scaling NeRF to render city-scale scenes spanning multiple blocks, it is vital to decompose the scene into individually trained NeRFs. This decomposition decouples rendering time from scene size, enables rendering to scale to arbitrarily large environments, and allows per-block updates of the environment. We adopt several architectural changes to make NeRF robust to data captured over months under different environmental conditions. We add appearance embeddings, learned pose refinement, and controllable exposure to each individual NeRF, and introduce a procedure for aligning appearance between adjacent NeRFs so that they can be seamlessly combined. We build a grid of Block-NeRFs from 2.8 million images to create the largest neural scene representation to date, capable of rendering an entire neighborhood of San Francisco.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 Block-NeRF，它是神经辐射场的一种变体，可以代表大规模环境。具体来说，我们证明了当缩放 NeRF 以渲染跨越多个街区的城市规模场景时，将场景分解为单独训练的 NeRF 至关重要。这种分解将渲染时间与场景大小分离，使渲染能够扩展到任意大的环境，并允许环境的每块更新。我们采用了多项架构更改，使 NeRF 对不同环境条件下数月内捕获的数据具有鲁棒性。我们为每个单独的 NeRF 添加了外观嵌入、学习姿势细化和可控曝光，并引入了对齐相邻 NeRF 之间外观的程序，以便它们可以无缝组合。我们利用 280 万张图像构建了一个 Block-NeRF 网格，以创建迄今为止最大的神经场景表示，能够渲染旧金山的整个社区。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Block-NeRF，一种可扩展的大规模场景神经视图合成方法，通过将场景分解为独立训练的NeRF块来实现城市级场景的渲染。
AI.motivation: 传统NeRF在处理大规模场景时存在渲染时间与场景大小强相关、无法更新环境等缺陷，需分解场景并增强模型鲁棒性以适应长期多变的环境条件。
AI.method: 将场景划分为独立训练的NeRF块，每个块包含外观嵌入、姿态优化和可控曝光模块，并引入相邻块外观对齐机制，最终通过280万张图像构建网格化表示。
AI.result: 创建了迄今为止最大的神经场景表示，成功渲染了旧金山整个社区的场景，验证了方法在大规模环境下的可行性。
AI.conclusion: Block-NeRF通过模块化设计解决了大规模场景的表示与渲染问题，实现了渲染时间与场景规模解耦及动态环境更新能力。
